Flight attendant contract ratification could reshape labor negotiations across major carriers and influence pilot contract strategies currently under discussion.

Premium seat investment competition among US carriers is intensifying as airlines prioritize cabin product differentiation to justify higher business class fares and capture corporate travel spending.

Star Alliance opens new Connection Centre at LAX to speed up tight transfers

Star Alliance has opened a new Star Connection Center at Los Angeles International Airport (LAX), its ninth such…...

Star Alliance's ninth Connection Center at LAX targets operational efficiency by streamlining the physical and procedural bottlenecks that plague tight connections, directly impacting airline profitability and network reliability.

NTSB's visual evidence documentation sets investigative precedent for understanding catastrophic accident sequences that will inform future aviation safety protocols.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

FAA investigates United Airlines 737 and Black Hawk close call in Santa Ana

A California National Guard UH-60M Black Hawk helicopter crossed in front of a United Airlines Boeing 737-800 on…...

The incident highlights critical gaps in low-altitude airspace coordination between military helicopter operations and commercial traffic near busy airports.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS frameworks shift aviation from accident investigation to systematic hazard identification, reducing incident likelihood before accidents occur.

US diverts Swiss F-35 funds to cover frozen Patriot payments

The United States has circumvented a Swiss payment freeze on the Patriot air defense system by redirecting funds…...

US procurement flexibility demonstrates how geopolitical constraints force creative financial routing in major defense contracts, potentially setting precedent for future allied military sales.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Boeing Boost: Major African Carrier Doubles Down On 737 MAX With 10 New Jets

The new aircraft will come quicker than you might expect.

African carriers' accelerating 737 MAX adoption signals regional confidence in the aircraft's recovery and production ramp-up timeline.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit

SAN FRANCISCO – Canadian startup SBQuantum plans to send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit March 30 on a Spire Global satellite flying on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare. Spire is providing the satellite, ground stations and data processing for SBQuantum’s magnetometer, which is...

Quantum sensing technology is moving from laboratory demonstrations to operational space assets, enabling new Earth observation capabilities through rideshare launch economics.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service

Japanese company ispace is revising its lunar lander design and further delaying the first mission by its American subsidiary while also unveiling plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

ispace's pivot to offering lunar communications infrastructure signals a shift from pure lander operations toward enabling broader commercial space economy participation.

See SpaceNews →

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Part 5 safety risk assessments require systematic evaluation methods beyond intuition, enabling operators to identify hazards and mitigation strategies with regulatory accountability.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Qantas Scraps Nonstop Perth-Paris Route In Major European Network Shake-Up

Qantas shakes up its international network to offer more flights to Europe via Singapore.

Qantas is consolidating long-haul capacity through Singapore hub rather than investing in ultra-long-range flights, signaling a shift toward network efficiency over point-to-point connectivity.

The union representing around 5,000 workers building the F-35 at Lockheed Martin’s Fort Worth, Texas, plant has opened…...

Labor negotiations at a major F-35 production facility could impact defense procurement timelines and fighter jet delivery schedules globally.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The MD-11 & DC-10

Though closely related, the MD-11 and DC-10 differed in range, cockpit design, aerodynamics, and ultimately commercial success.

The MD-11's advanced avionics and extended range couldn't overcome aerodynamic challenges that limited its adoption compared to the DC-10's proven reliability in cargo operations.

See Simple Flying →

London, England — TEKEVER, a leading provider of advanced AI-based autonomous systems, and Quadsat have successfully...

Integration of EW payloads onto commercial drone platforms enables operators to conduct spectrum surveillance and threat detection missions without specialized military aircraft.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain lethally underestimated by helicopter pilots who overestimate their reaction time, making pre-flight route planning and low-altitude awareness critical survival skills.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The US Domestic Routes Operated By Widebody Aircraft

Some of the country's busiest domestic routes are operated by widebodies.

Widebody aircraft on domestic routes indicate transcontinental capacity constraints and higher per-seat operating costs that suggest strong demand justifies inefficient fleet deployment.

United Airlines Jet & Military Helicopter Separated By Only 500 Feet Over Orange County

A close call in the sunny skies of SoCal.

Near-miss incidents at lower altitudes suggest potential gaps in coordination between civilian and military airspace management procedures in congested regions.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Satellite Spots a Spawn

The activity of herring around Vancouver Island in British Columbia brightened coastal waters enough to be detectable from space.

Satellite remote sensing technology demonstrates unprecedented sensitivity in detecting biological phenomena, expanding potential applications beyond traditional Earth observation for environmental monitoring.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Range The Airbus A350-1000 Has Compared To The Boeing 777-300ER

The Airbus A350-1000 and Boeing 777-300ER are long-haul aviation titans, but the A350's slight edge in range may not be as decisive as it seems.

While the A350-1000's range advantage appears marginal on paper, operational factors like fuel efficiency and payload flexibility may prove more strategically valuable for route planning than raw distance capability.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit confusion at LAX demonstrates how high-traffic airport design can create systematic incursion risks even for experienced crews operating familiar routes.

AIR claims $1 billion in orders as eVTOL maker pushes personal aircraft

Israel-based electric aircraft developer AIR has declared that it has surpassed $1 billion in orders, driven by demand…...

AIR's billion-dollar order book signals that pre-certification customer commitment is becoming a viable funding mechanism for eVTOL manufacturers facing extended certification timelines.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The GE9X Is Compared To The GE90

The engine is set to enter commercial service on the upcoming Boeing 777X.

The GE9X's enlarged design reflects how modern wide-body aircraft demand proportionally massive engines to achieve fuel efficiency and range improvements over previous generations.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft

Commercial space tracking data shows U.S. satellites coordinating maneuvers to maintain proximity and continuous observation of a pair of Chinese spacecraft in geostationary orbit.

U.S. space surveillance coordination demonstrates evolving operational tactics for monitoring potential anti-satellite threats in contested GEO environment.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

8 Hours In A Boeing 737 MAX? Inside United Airlines' New Longest Nonstop Flights

Half of the ten longest routes started in 2025 or will do so in 2026. See them all here!

United's expansion of ultra-long 737 MAX routes signals airlines' confidence in the aircraft's reliability and fuel efficiency for transcontinental operations post-crisis.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 27, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Airlines can simultaneously increase capacity and reduce emissions by optimizing operational procedures across all three layers—aircraft, airport, and airspace—rather than waiting for new aircraft technology alone.

See MDPI →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

I'm A Flight Attendant, And These Are The Things I Wish Every Passenger Knew Before Boarding

Practical advice for passengers that makes flights easier.

Flight attendants report that passenger compliance with pre-flight briefings and basic cabin procedures significantly reduces operational delays and safety risks during flights.

Space Command begins phased move to Alabama

The new headquarters building is expected to be completed by 2031...

Space Command's relocation to Alabama represents a significant infrastructure shift that will influence regional aerospace supply chains and defense contractor partnerships through the 2030s.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA

Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

April's astronomical events, including peak meteor activity and a bright comet, require advance crew awareness planning for potential navigation and visibility impacts during night operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa

Se invita a los medios de comunicación el martes 21 de abril al Centro de Vuelo Espacial Goddard de la NASA en Greenbelt, Maryland, para conocer el telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la agencia, cuya construcción terminó recientemente y el cual se encuentra ultimando las pruebas previas a su...

NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope completion marks a significant advancement in space-based astronomy capabilities with implications for future aerospace missions and scientific discovery.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space

While government agencies are increasingly turning to commercial contracting mechanisms for space capabilities, that does not necessarily translate to additional business opportunities for the companies offering them.

Government space procurement contracts may not expand market opportunities for commercial providers despite agencies adopting commercial contracting models.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing

Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully...

Roman's completion marks a critical pre-launch milestone that will enable unprecedented high-resolution imaging for exoplanet detection and dark energy research upon its scheduled deployment.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown

Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about...

Artemis II's multi-national crew composition signals aerospace industry demand for international cooperation on lunar missions and expanded supply chain opportunities.

See NASA →

Navi AI launches publicly with $6 million to expand AI pilot training platform

Navi AI has launched publicly with more than $6 million in funding, as it looks to expand its…...

Navi AI's public launch signals investor confidence in AI-driven pilot training as a scalable alternative to traditional flight schools, potentially reshaping how airlines recruit and develop crews.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager

As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers,...

Specialized thermal management expertise is now mission-critical for ISS resupply operations, requiring dedicated coordination between ground teams and payload developers.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Turning growth into profits remains a challenge as space demand grows

While the space economy is expanding across multiple fronts, questions remain about how some of its most promising opportunities could translate growing demand into sustainable profits.

Space sector growth is outpacing profitability, signaling that emerging operators must develop differentiated business models before market consolidation pressures margins.
